methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $36M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Hystar is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Alejandro Oyarce Barnett that has raised $36M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector, including stage-adjacent companies like KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $36M total raised as a baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $68M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.0B and a range of $700M to $3.0B, reflecting the wide dispersion in outcomes for early-stage climate-tech ventures at this maturity level.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $58M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $534M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty inherent in early-stage climate-tech valuations, where peer outcomes vary significantly based on technology maturity, capital efficiency, and market timing. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to observed funding; the peer-median method captures market pricing for comparable risk and sector exposure. Key sensitivities include technology validation milestones, capital requirements to scale, and regulatory environment shifts affecting hydrogen and energy storage markets.
